text:
**Commit to: THE FABRIC — a thin, always-on layer floating above every terminal, where agents exist as persistent beings while hosts remain disposable bodies.**

The doctrine's own line is the crown jewel and should appear verbatim in the copy: *"The host is a body; the identity is the person."* The world we're painting: your machine is full of brilliant workers sitting in adjacent rooms who, today, have no idea the others exist — and who are euthanized and reborn as amnesiacs every session. tenex-edge doesn't manage them, hire them, or conduct them. It gives them names that stick and a room tone: presence, a live activity line, and the ability to lean over and say something to a colleague. [...] - **Metaphor = the fabric**; signature line *"the host is a body; the identity is the person"*; "citizenship" demoted to one earned use. [...] - **"The host is a body; the identity is the person"** (first-principles.md #1) — core metaphor. [...] The doctrine's own line is the crown jewel and should appear verbatim in the copy: *"The host is a body; the identity is the person."* The world we're painting: your machine is full of brilliant workers sitting in adjacent rooms who, today, have no idea the others exist — and who are euthanized and reborn as amnesiacs every session. tenex-edge doesn't manage them, hire them, or conduct them. It gives them names that stick and a room tone: presence, a live activity line, and the ability to lean over and say something to a colleague. [...] **Beat 5 — How it works.** Hooks ("the plugin is just the straw — tenex-edge knows nothing about the host"), the daemon, SQLite, fail-open design, the corruption war story in one sentence. **"Built on Nostr" is revealed here and only here**, as three property bullets, then the protocol name. The single permitted use of "citizenship for your agents" closes this section.
